rationale: The cited 2015 JMR paper appears in grouped citations as background support, mainly to illustrate prior service-performance work and prior VAR-style modeling of asymmetric effects. The citing paper does not reuse that paper's DASVAR specification, crisis mechanism, or core empirical claims in its own hypotheses or tests. Instead, it develops a different substantive setting (spotlight personnel in soccer) and positions its own LCP approach against a broader set of prior methods. This indicates a passing positioning/reference role rather than a direct substantive dependency.
